prompt_text:
Analyze the following java source code repository for violations of the Single Responsibility Principle (SRP).

## Principle Definition
A class should have only one reason to change. It should encapsulate exactly one responsibility. When a class handles multiple unrelated concerns (e.g., data processing AND rendering AND persistence), it violates SRP.

## What to Look For
- Classes that handle multiple unrelated responsibilities
- Methods that mix business logic with I/O, formatting, or persiste...
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/json/UTF8StreamJsonParser.java",
      "entity_name": "UTF8StreamJsonParser",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 305,
      "line_end": 416,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Parser class implements complex Base64 decoding logic internally.",
      "reasoning": "The primary responsibility of UTF8StreamJsonParser is to tokenize JSON text from a byte stream. However, it also encapsulates the detailed logic for decoding Base64-encoded string content into binary data (methods _readBinary and _decodeBase64). Base64 decoding is a distinct data transformation concern that should be handled by a separate codec or utility class, as changes to Base64 requirements or the addition of new variants would force changes to the parser class."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/json/UTF8JsonGenerator.java",
      "entity_name": "UTF8JsonGenerator",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 1056,
      "line_end": 1180,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Generator class implements complex Base64 encoding logic internally.",
      "reasoning": "The responsibility of UTF8JsonGenerator is to produce valid JSON syntax and encode characters into UTF-8. By including the logic for converting raw binary data into Base64-encoded strings (_writeBinary methods), it takes on a secondary responsibility of data encoding/formatting. This logic is unrelated to JSON structural generation and increases the complexity and maintenance surface of the generator."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/JsonGenerator.java",
      "entity_name": "JsonGenerator.writeTypePrefix",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 1040,
      "line_end": 1128,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "The base generator class handles polymorphic type metadata wrapping logic.",
      "reasoning": "The JsonGenerator is intended to be a low-level API for streaming JSON tokens. The methods writeTypePrefix, _writeTypePrefixUsingNative, and _writeTypePrefixUsingWrapper implement high-level logic for polymorphic type handling (e.g., deciding between WRAPPER_ARRAY, WRAPPER_OBJECT, or METADATA_PROPERTY). This responsibility belongs to a higher-level data-binding or metadata-management layer, not the low-level token stream generator."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/json/UTF8DataInputJsonParser.java",
      "entity_name": "UTF8DataInputJsonParser",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 244,
      "line_end": 360,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Parser class duplicates and encapsulates Base64 decoding logic for DataInput sources.",
      "reasoning": "Similar to the stream-based parser, this class mixes the responsibility of parsing JSON grammar with the responsibility of decoding Base64 binary data. The implementation of _readBinary is tightly coupled to the DataInput source, making the decoding logic difficult to reuse or test independently of the parsing process."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/json/ReaderBasedJsonParser.java",
      "entity_name": "ReaderBasedJsonParser",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 137,
      "line_end": 170,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "low",
      "description": "Parser class manages low-level I/O resource lifecycle and buffer recycling.",
      "reasoning": "The class is responsible for parsing JSON tokens, but it also manages the lifecycle of the underlying java.io.Reader (closing it based on configuration) and the recycling of character buffers through the IOContext. While common in performance-oriented Java code, resource management and grammar parsing are technically two different reasons for a class to change."
    }
  ]
}
```
